blocks.title: Device Constraints
body: The target display is 800 x 480 px in landscape orientation.
There are five physical reader ports below the screen, so the UI often needs to show both the on-screen workflow state and the physical cassette state at the same time.
Assume this is an Android device. Where possible, use standard Android behavior for the keyboard, date and time pickers, navigation, and opening new screens instead of redesigning those patterns.
bullets: If a screen can fit without scrolling, that is preferred.
Scrolling should be limited as much as possible because the touchscreen does not scroll well.
The most important actions and the port status should be readable at a glance.

blocks.title: Normal Test
body: A normal test is the standard workflow for a milk sample. The user chooses a port, enters sample and operator information, selects or confirms the test type, and starts the read.
Some tests are read only. Other tests need incubation before reading.
bullets: Configuration can happen before the cassette is inserted.
The test type can be chosen manually or prefilled from QR.
The main goal is to help the operator prepare the correct test on the correct port with as little friction as possible.

blocks.title: Individual Test vs Test Flow
body: An individual test is one single cassette read. It always describes the result of that one cassette only.
A test flow is the whole sequence for one sample on one port. A flow can contain one test, two tests, or three tests depending on whether confirmation is needed.

definitions.label: Individual negative
definitions.description: One cassette read came out negative.

definitions.label: Individual positive
definitions.description: One cassette read came out positive.

definitions.label: Flow negative
definitions.description: The whole sample flow ended negative, even if more than one cassette was used.

definitions.label: Flow positive
definitions.description: The whole sample flow ended positive, even if more than one cassette was used.

definitions.label: Flow inconclusive
definitions.description: The operator aborted the confirmation flow before it reached a final positive or negative end.

blocks.title: Test Labels Used In History
body: The interface uses short names like T1, T2, and T3 in compact places such as buttons and dashboard cards.
In history and detail views, the same three steps are labeled with longer names so the user can see where each cassette belongs in the full sample flow.

definitions.label: Original
definitions.description: The first cassette read for the sample. This is the same step the dashboard often calls T1.

definitions.label: First Confirmation
definitions.description: The second cassette read for the same sample. This is the same step the dashboard often calls T2.

definitions.label: Second Confirmation
definitions.description: The third cassette read for the same sample. This is the same step the dashboard often calls T3.

bullets: Original = Test 1 = T1
First Confirmation = Test 2 = T2
Second Confirmation = Test 3 = T3

blocks.title: Confirmation Flow
body: Confirmation starts only when the first individual test is positive. If the first individual test is negative, the whole flow finishes immediately and no confirmation modal is shown.
From there, the operator can continue to the next test or abort the flow. Aborting at any confirmation step leads to an inconclusive flow result.
In the dashboard, the steps are often shortened to T1, T2, and T3. In history, the same steps appear as Original, First Confirmation, and Second Confirmation.
bullets: Flow negative can happen after 1 test or after 3 tests.
Flow positive can happen after 2 tests or after 3 tests.
Flow inconclusive happens only when the operator aborts the confirmation flow.
Test 2 and Test 3 always require a newly inserted cassette.
The next cassette must be the same test type as the original test.

next.label: Insert new cassette and run T2 / First Confirmation
Insert new cassette and run T3 / Second Confirmation

blocks.title: Controls
body: Positive controls and animal controls are not normal sample tests. They are special runs where a positive result is expected.
They should stay clearly separated from normal tests in both the workflow and the history.

blocks.title: Verification
body: Verification is a service workflow used to check port performance and reading behavior.
The operator places a physical thermometer into the port, measures the real temperature, and compares it to the selected 40 C or 50 C target.
The result also shows values such as light intensity and line ratios. These help show how well the device can read the cassette lines.
bullets: All ports always share the same reader temperature setting.
The reader should be verified every 250 total tests across all ports.
The user can set a lower local warning threshold, but the cloud default stays 250.
After verification, the shared reader counter resets to zero.

blocks.title: Cassettes, Strips, and QR
body: The device supports both strip tests and cassette tests. Strips are the older format and do not have QR. Cassettes are the newer format and can have QR.
When QR is enabled and the cassette supports it, the device reads the test type from QR and does not let the user change it.
This matters because different test types can have different substances, different cassette layouts, and different numbers of lines.
During confirmation flows, the device also checks that the next cassette is the same test type as the original test.
If QR is turned off, the user must choose the test type manually.
bullets: A batch means a box of many cassettes of the same type.
The slot can show the control line and the test lines for the inserted cassette.
Test lines can use short substance abbreviations because some tests have fewer lines and some have more.
The control line is always present because it shows whether the test itself worked.

blocks.title: Microswitch
body: The microswitch is the cassette insertion sensor.
Its main job is to detect that a cassette was inserted after configuration so the read action can be enabled.
Its limitation is that it can reliably detect insertion, but not removal.
When it is on, the read buttons unlock only after cassette insertion is detected. When it is off, the device allows a fallback mode without that check, for example if the sensor is broken.

blocks.title: Account and Settings Access
body: The device can be signed in to the cloud or used in anonymous mode.
In anonymous mode, test records are not sent to the cloud.
When the device is signed in, the allowed test types come from the backend. When it is anonymous, the user can turn test types on and off locally in Settings.
bullets: Some settings are open to normal users.
Some settings require the reader password.
After the password is entered, protected settings stay unlocked while Settings remains open.

blocks.title: Other Important Concepts
bullets: Some tests require a specific reader temperature, usually 40 C or 50 C.
Quantitative tests need a loaded quant curve before they can start.
History separates normal tests and controls.
Exports are CSV or LIMS, not Excel.
Root full-screen screens use Close, while pushed child screens use Back.

items: Expanded the History handoff coverage with additional list, flow detail, test detail, and export states.
Added tall History detail captures so scrollable flow and test screens export at full height for Figma handoff.
Removed the flow-level CSV export action from the History source UI and corrected the empty controls copy to say no control records exist on the reader yet.
Trimmed redundant History handoff states after review, while keeping the controls list state so the blue control rows remain documented.

sections.title: Five-Port Dashboard
sections.summary: Core dashboard states grouped by setup, selection, quant workflow, confirmation, and completion.

subsections.title: Configuration
subsections.summary: How a port is prepared before reading, including QR prefill, cassette gating, and temperature checks.

subsections.title: Test Selection
subsections.summary: How manual test type selection looks across the different test families and badge combinations.

subsections.title: Quant Curve Loading
subsections.summary: How quantitative tests block, load, and confirm a required quant curve before reading.

subsections.title: Confirmation Flow
subsections.summary: How Test 2 and Test 3 are guided, including fresh cassette requirements and cassette mismatch edge cases.

subsections.title: Read, Results, and Errors
subsections.summary: What active processing, retryable errors, and completed flows look like on the live dashboard.

subsections.title: Flow Details
subsections.summary: The long scrollable detail modal that opens from View Details after a completed run.

states.title: Empty dashboard
states.description: All five channels idle with Configure available.

states.title: Cassette detected
states.description: Inserted QR cassette is detected before configuration.

states.title: Strip detected
states.description: Inserted strip is detected before configuration, but the test type is not prefilled because strips do not have QR.

states.title: Configure before insert
states.description: Manual configuration with microswitch on and read disabled until cassette insertion.

states.title: QR-locked configuration
states.description: Inserted QR cassette auto-fills the test type and locks editing.

states.title: Microswitch fallback
states.description: Microswitch off leaves reading available without cassette gating.

states.title: Temperature not correct
states.description: Selected test needs a different reader temperature, so reading is blocked until the temperature matches.

states.title: Test type disabled in profile
states.description: Signed-in reader profile blocks the selected test type, so the configuration form shows a blocking error.

states.title: Choose test type
states.description: Manual test type picker showing different badge combinations across qualitative, quantitative, strip, and cassette tests.

states.title: QR cassette inserted, QR off
states.description: A QR-capable cassette is inserted, but QR scanning is disabled, so the operator must choose the test type manually.

states.title: Choose test type: quantitative
states.description: Manual test type picker filtered to quantitative test types so the Quant badge is visible.

states.title: Quant curve required
states.description: Quantitative strip selected without a loaded curve.

states.title: Load quant curve
states.description: Quant curve picker opened from the dashboard configuration flow.

states.title: Decision after Test 1 positive
states.description: Confirmation modal prompting the operator to continue with Test 2.

states.title: Ready for Test 2
states.description: Confirmation flow waiting for a newly inserted cassette.

states.title: Test 2 cassette ready
states.description: Fresh cassette inserted and the confirmation flow is ready to continue.

states.title: Decision after Test 2 negative
states.description: Tie-breaker modal prompting the operator to continue with Test 3.

states.title: Abort flow confirmation
states.description: Abort-flow modal summarizing the completed tests before the operator confirms an inconclusive result.

states.title: Ready for Test 3
states.description: Tie-breaker flow waiting for a newly inserted cassette before Test 3 can start.

states.title: Test 3 cassette ready
states.description: Fresh cassette inserted and the tie-breaker flow is ready to continue.

states.title: Confirmation error: wrong cassette
states.description: Replacement cassette does not match the expected test type.

states.title: Confirmation error: used cassette
states.description: Replacement cassette was already used, so the operator must insert a new one.

states.title: Incubating
states.description: Read plus incubate flow with countdown and abort action.

states.title: Reading cassette
states.description: Active read in progress after the cassette has been accepted and the run has started.

states.title: Read error: no cassette
states.description: Unified red error state shown when a read starts without a readable cassette.

states.title: Read error: incubation interrupted
states.description: Incubation was stopped by the user, so the same retry-or-abort error state is shown.

states.title: Completed negative flow
states.description: Single-test flow completed negative without opening a confirmation modal.

states.title: Completed positive flow
states.description: Confirmation flow resolved positive after the second test.

states.title: Completed inconclusive flow
states.description: Confirmation flow aborted after conflicting results.

states.title: Positive control: positive result
states.description: Positive control completed with the expected positive result.

states.title: Positive control: negative result
states.description: Positive control completed with an unexpected negative result.

states.title: Animal control: positive result
states.description: Animal control completed with a positive result so the dashboard shows the distinct control labeling.

states.title: Flow detail: negative result
states.description: Detailed modal for a completed single-test negative flow, including the full scrollable substance area.

states.title: Flow detail: confirmed positive
states.description: Detailed modal for a completed positive confirmation flow with both contained tests visible.

states.title: Flow detail: inconclusive
states.description: Detailed modal for an inconclusive confirmation flow after conflicting test results.

states.title: Flow detail: positive control positive
states.description: Detailed modal for a positive control with the expected positive result.

states.title: Flow detail: positive control negative
states.description: Detailed modal for a positive control with an unexpected negative result.

subsections.summary: Tests and controls filters, pagination states, and empty placeholders.

states.title: History list: tests

states.description: Records exist on the reader, but the selected controls filter has no matches and shows the filtered empty placeholder.

states.title: Flow detail: positive

states.title: Flow detail: negative

states.title: Flow detail: inconclusive

states.title: Flow detail: positive control

states.title: Flow detail: animal control negative
states.description: Tall capture of a negative animal control flow so the control layout is documented with a negative result badge.

states.title: Flow detail: edit comment
states.description: Tall capture of the inline flow comment editor with textarea plus save and cancel actions.

states.title: Test detail: positive
states.description: Tall capture of a positive individual test with qualitative substance results and light-intensity chart.

states.title: Test detail: negative
states.description: Tall capture of a negative individual test from a confirmation sequence.

states.title: Test detail: quantitative
states.description: Tall capture of a quantitative test so the measured-value layout is included in the handoff.

states.title: Test detail: rejected
states.description: Tall capture of a rejected test record so the alternate annotation label is documented.

states.title: Test detail: positive control
states.description: Tall capture of an archived positive control test detail.

states.title: Test detail: animal control negative

states.title: Export modal

sections.title: Verification
sections.summary: Verification setup, guided run, result detail, and verification archive.

states.title: Verification setup
states.description: Choose the verification port and target temperature before starting verification.

states.title: Verification warmup heating
states.description: Reader is heating the selected port to the verification target temperature.

states.title: Verification warmup ready
states.description: Reader reached target temperature and is ready for thermometer insertion.

states.title: Measured temperature
states.description: Operator enters the thermometer reading.

states.title: Measured temperature: next disabled
states.description: The measured-temperature step keeps the next action disabled until the operator enters a valid thermometer reading.

states.title: Insert verification asset: waiting
states.description: Insert step before the cassette is detected, with Start Verification Test disabled.

states.title: Insert verification asset: detected
states.description: Insert step after the cassette is detected and the verification read can be started.

states.title: Insert verification asset: read failed
states.description: Insert step shown after a failed read attempt, with guidance to reinsert the cassette and try again.

states.title: Verification reading: scanning
states.description: Initial loading stage while the reader scans the verification cassette.

states.title: Verification reading
states.description: Verification read is in progress.

states.title: Verification result detail
states.description: Detailed verification result with pass and fail criteria.

states.title: Verification result detail: passed live run
states.description: Successful verification result opened directly after a live run, including the post-run footer actions.

states.title: Verification result detail: light intensity fail
states.description: Failed verification result where only the light-intensity check fails.

states.title: Verification result detail: info overlay
states.description: Verification result detail with the help overlay open and all three pass criteria explained.

states.title: Verification history
states.description: List of previously saved verification runs.

states.title: Verification history: empty
states.description: Empty-state history screen shown before any verification run has been saved on the reader.
